title: HistoricalEpisode
description: Synthetic episode payload produced by Stage 0.5 (ingest-history.mjs) and consumed by the historical distiller adapter (contract-ide/src-tauri/src/distiller/historical.rs). One episode per discovered artifact (CLAUDE.md file, single commit, single ADR, single CHANGELOG entry). Distinct shape from session episodes — no turn refs, no actor attribution; provenance carries the artifact's file path / commit SHA so substrate_nodes get anchored to a stable source identity for re-runs and supersession.

artifact_type.description: Drives which extraction-rule branch the distiller prompt applies. Must match one of the 5 supported artifact types.

content_text.description: The artifact's verbatim text. For commits: subject + '\n\n' + body. For files (CLAUDE.md / ADR / CHANGELOG / DECISIONS.md): full file contents OR a single section if the file is long enough to warrant chunking. minLength 20 prevents distillation of empty / trivial inputs at the schema layer.

content_hash.description: sha256(content_text) hex-encoded. Drives Stage 0.5 hash-skip — re-runs of ingest-history.mjs check this against existing .staging/historical-episodes.jsonl entries to skip already-distilled artifacts.

source.description: Discriminator separating synthetic-episode flow from live-session episode flow. The Rust adapter (distiller/historical.rs) keys off this value when persisting to substrate_nodes.

repo_path.description: Absolute path to the target repo's root. Persisted into substrate_node provenance so retrieval can scope to the originating repo.

file_path.description: For artifact_types claude_md / adr / changelog / decisions_md: repo-relative path of the source file. ABSENT for commits.

commit_sha.description: For artifact_type=commit: the commit SHA (full or short). ABSENT for file-based artifacts.

commit_subject.description: For artifact_type=commit: the commit subject line, used to render substrate_node.source_quote when LLM output omits a quote.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the artifact (commit author date for commits; file mtime or first-seen date for files). Used for ordering when multiple artifacts produce conflicting decisions — most-recent-wins fallback in supersession.

examples.content_text:
## Decisions

- Soft-delete for notes, hard-delete for users with Stripe customer archive (not delete) for tax record retention.
- Stripe webhook idempotency via event.id — every webhook handler upserts on (event_id, processed_at).
- Resend for transactional, no marketing list yet — welcome email on signup is the only template.

feat: argon2 sessions + login/signup/logout (lucia v3 is eol)

Lucia v3 hit EOL and the @lucia-auth/adapter-prisma peer-deps block Prisma 7. Rolled argon2id sessions in-house — same surface as Lucia (login/signup/logout + getSession), ~80 lines in src/lib/auth.ts. Trade-off accepted: we maintain the password hashing + session table directly, but unblock Prisma 7 today.

provenance.commit_subject: feat: argon2 sessions + login/signup/logout (lucia v3 is eol)

examples.content_text:
chore: bump deps to latest minor versions

Routine maintenance.

provenance.commit_subject: chore: bump deps to latest minor versions
